programs.name: Health First Colorado Buy-In (QMB, SLMB, QI)
programs.what_it_does: These Medicare Savings Programs under Health First Colorado pay Medicare premiums, deductibles, and copays for low-income seniors. QMB covers Part A/B premiums fully; SLMB/QI cover Part B premiums.
programs.who_its_for: Seniors age 65+ on Medicare with incomes up to 100-135% FPL and limited assets.
programs.benefit_value: QMB: Full Part A/B costs; SLMB: Part B premium (~$174.70/month 2024); QI: Partial premium assistance.

programs.name: Home and Community-Based Services (HCBS) Waivers
programs.what_it_does: These Medicaid waivers under Health First Colorado fund home- and community-based services to avoid institutional care. They cover in-home care, adult day services, respite, home modifications, and assisted living costs.
programs.who_its_for: Seniors age 65+ or disabled with income ≤300% Federal Benefit Rate (~$3,200/month individual), assets ≤$2,000.
programs.benefit_value: Up to $1,500/month (Home Care Allowance); one-time grants up to $1,200/year (Community Access Services) for repairs, utilities, equipment.

programs.name: Program of All-Inclusive Care for the Elderly (PACE)
programs.what_it_does: PACE provides comprehensive medical and social services to frail seniors in community settings, including adult day health, transportation, and home care. It coordinates all Medicare and Medicaid-covered care through interdisciplinary teams.
programs.who_its_for: Seniors age 55+ certified nursing home eligible, able to live safely in community, enrolled in Medicare/Medicaid.
programs.benefit_value: Full coverage of approved services at no additional cost beyond Medicare/Medicaid premiums.

programs.name: Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP)
programs.what_it_does: SNAP provides monthly benefits via EBT card for purchasing groceries to improve food security. It frees up income for other senior care expenses.
programs.who_its_for: Seniors age 60+ with gross income ≤130% FPL (~$1,730/month individual), net income/assets limits ($3,750 non-vehicle).
programs.benefit_value: Average $200-300/month per eligible senior household (varies by income/size).

programs.name: Low-Income Energy Assistance Program (LEAP)
programs.what_it_does: LEAP provides grants to help pay winter heating bills and year-round energy costs. It assists with past-due utilities and crisis intervention.
programs.who_its_for: Households age 60+ with income ≤60% state median (~$3,448/month family of 1), high energy burden.

programs.name: Weatherization Assistance Program (WAP)
programs.what_it_does: WAP funds home energy efficiency improvements like insulation, sealing, and furnace repairs to reduce utility costs. It includes health/safety measures for low-income homes.
programs.benefit_value: Free services; average savings $300+/year on energy bills.

programs.name: State Health Insurance Assistance Program (SHIP)
programs.what_it_does: Colorado SHIP offers free counseling on Medicare options, supplements, and assistance programs. Counselors help with plan selection, appeals, and fraud prevention.
programs.who_its_for: All Medicare beneficiaries age 65+, caregivers, family members.

programs.name: Meals on Wheels Colorado
programs.what_it_does: Provides nutritious home-delivered meals to homebound seniors to promote independence. Includes safety check-ins and optional friendly visits.
programs.who_its_for: Homebound seniors age 60+ unable to prepare meals, often low-income.
programs.benefit_value: 5-14 meals/week; suggested donation $3-6/meal, free/subsidized for low-income.

programs.name: Alzheimer’s Disease and Related D Dementia (ADRD) Respite
programs.what_it_does: Offers respite care services to relieve family caregivers of dementia patients. Includes in-home, adult day, or short-term facility care.
programs.who_its_for: Unpaid caregivers of seniors with Alzheimer’s/dementia.
programs.benefit_value: Up to $1,500/year per caregiver via multipurpose senior services.

programs.name: Senior Community Service Employment Program (SCSEP)
programs.what_it_does: Provides part-time paid community service work training leading to unsubsidized employment. Includes job placement support for older workers.
programs.benefit_value: Minimum wage work ~20 hours/week; supportive services.

programs.name: Colorado Legal Services (Seniors)
programs.what_it_does: Provides free legal aid for income-eligible seniors on issues like housing, benefits, abuse, and consumer problems. Includes advice, representation, and education.
programs.benefit_value: Free legal services valued at thousands per case.

programs.name: Long-Term Care Ombudsman Program
programs.what_it_does: Advocates for residents in long-term care facilities resolving complaints and protecting rights. Provides education and monitoring for quality care.
programs.who_its_for: Residents/patients in nursing homes, assisted living, and similar facilities; families.
programs.benefit_value: Free advocacy services resolving 80%+ of complaints.
